events.name: Webbed Snare
events.gmPrompt: Something has been building in the canopy above camp. The silk is thick, structural, anchored to multiple trees. This is not old webbing. The builder is close.
events.checkContext: Can the watch spot the spider before it descends?
events.gmGuidance: This is a real spider, big enough to be a threat. Not a swarm, not combat-scale -- think a bold hunting spider the size of a dog. It retreats when hurt. The danger is the ambush bite, not a prolonged fight.
events.description: Thick silk anchored across multiple trees above camp. The builder is close.

onTriumph.narrative: Spotted the spider before it descended. Torchlight drove it back into the canopy. Camp repositioned, rest undisturbed.

onSuccess.narrative: Heard it moving overhead. Noise and fire drove it off. A tense hour, but no contact.

onMixed.narrative: It dropped on the edge of camp before anyone reacted. Fangs caught a bedroll, not skin. Driven off with burns, but the scare costs everyone sleep.

onFailure.narrative: It got a sleeper. A bite before anyone could react, then it fled into the canopy when the camp woke screaming.

effects.description: Venom from spider bite. DC 11 CON save or poisoned until dawn.

events.name: Hidden Spring
events.gmPrompt: Water bubbling up from between roots. Cold, clear, and running over clean stone.
events.checkContext: Can the watch identify the spring as safe and useful?
events.gmGuidance: Discovery only fires if watch is set. The spring is always safe to drink from; the check determines how much the party benefits from the find.
events.description: Water bubbling up from between roots. Cold, clear, and running over clean stone.

onTriumph.narrative: Exceptionally pure water. Waterskins refilled, a pot of strong tea brewed. The minerals in the spring ease aching muscles. Everyone sleeps well.

onSuccess.narrative: Clean water. Waterskins refilled and a brew put on. Everyone sleeps a little easier.

onMixed.narrative: Drinkable water. Skins refilled but too dark to properly assess the spring. A welcome find regardless.

onFailure.narrative: The water looks clear but tastes faintly of minerals. Safe enough, but nothing special.

events.name: Night Owl
events.gmPrompt: A large owl watches from the lowest branch. Unblinking. Unhurried. It has been here longer than you have, and it will be here after you leave.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. The owl is real, mundane, and completely indifferent to the party. If a ranger or druid wants to commune with it, that is player-driven. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: A large owl on the lowest branch. Unblinking. Unhurried.

events.name: Fallen Giant
events.gmPrompt: A huge tree fell recently. The trunk is hollow, the wood inside dry. A windbreak and a fuel source in one stroke of luck.
events.checkContext: Can the watch harvest usable fuel from the fallen trunk?
events.gmGuidance: Discovery only fires if watch is set. The kindling is a tangible item the player receives. Useful for campfire minigame or trade. The windbreak narrative is flavour only.
events.description: A recently fallen tree. The trunk is hollow, the wood inside dry.

onTriumph.narrative: Dry firewood and a natural windbreak. The hollow trunk shelters the fire perfectly.

onSuccess.narrative: Good dry wood from the hollow. Enough to keep the fire going all night.

onMixed.narrative: Usable wood but the hollow is full of beetles. Manageable.

onFailure.narrative: The wood is punky and damp. Smokes badly. More nuisance than help.

events.name: Old Trap
events.gmPrompt: A click underfoot. Something mechanical shifts beneath the flagstones near the camp perimeter. Old, but not spent.
events.checkContext: Can the watch find and disarm the mechanism before it fires?
events.gmGuidance: Dungeon-specific hazard. Investigation, not Perception -- the trap is hidden in stonework, not visible. The mechanism is old and unreliable; even failure is a single bolt, not a room-clearing trap.
events.description: A click underfoot. Something mechanical shifts beneath the flagstones.

onTriumph.narrative: Disarmed cleanly. Recovered a spring-loaded bolt. Usable as an improvised alarm for the rest of the night.

onSuccess.narrative: Found and jammed the mechanism. It will not fire tonight.

onMixed.narrative: Triggered it at arm's length. A rusty bolt skitters across the floor. The noise echoes through the corridors. Sleep disrupted.

effects.description: Noise from triggered trap disrupted sleep.

onFailure.narrative: A bolt fires from a wall slot. Catches the watcher before they can dodge.

effects.description: Noise and injury disrupted the camp.

events.name: Dripping Ceiling
events.gmPrompt: A persistent drip from a crack in the ceiling. Steady, cold, and aimed squarely at the gear pile. The stone weeps.
events.checkContext: Can the watch redirect the drip before it soaks the gear?
events.gmGuidance: Structural groundwater, not a flood. The drip does not stop; this is the dungeon being a dungeon. Survival to read the stone and redirect.
events.description: A persistent drip from a crack in the ceiling. Aimed at the gear pile.

onTriumph.narrative: Channelled the drip with a cloak before it reached anything. The rhythmic sound was almost soothing.

onSuccess.narrative: Packs moved, drip redirected. Minor inconvenience, nothing lost.

onMixed.narrative: Most gear saved, but one bedroll caught the worst of it. Damp and cold by morning.

onFailure.narrative: The drip found the ration pack. Soaked through. Cold, damp, miserable.

events.name: Territorial Rams
events.gmPrompt: Hooves on rock above. Mountain goats, territorial and agitated near their grazing ledge. One paws at the ground and lowers its head.
events.checkContext: Can the watch calm or deter the rams before they charge?
events.gmGuidance: Animal Handling to de-escalate. These are territorial animals, not monsters. Giving ground works. auto_fail without a watch because the goats charge into an unguarded camp.
events.description: Mountain goats, territorial and agitated. One lowers its head.

onTriumph.narrative: You read their body language perfectly. Slow movements, low voice, no eye contact. They settled and drifted away within minutes.

onSuccess.narrative: You give ground slowly, hands open. They watch for a while, then lose interest and move on.

onMixed.narrative: They scattered eventually, but not before one clipped through the edge of camp. Gear knocked around, sleep disrupted.

onFailure.narrative: One charges through camp. Catches someone across the ribs before anyone can react. Gear knocked from the ledge.

effects.narrative: Gear knocked from the ledge by charging ram.

events.name: Hoarfrost Morning
events.gmPrompt: Everything is coated in ice crystals at dawn. Every blade of grass, every tent line, every eyelash. The camp sparkles in the first light. Cold, quiet, and perfectly still.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. Beautiful and cold. A moment of stillness before the day starts. If the party is worried about cold damage, this is not that. It is just frost. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Everything coated in ice crystals at dawn. The camp sparkles in the first light.

events.name: Rockfall
events.gmPrompt: A crack from above. Small stones skittering down the slope, bouncing off ledges. Something dislodged up there. The big rocks follow the small ones.
events.checkContext: Can the watch hear the warning signs and move the camp to safety?
events.gmGuidance: Loose scree from thermal contraction, not an avalanche. Perception to hear the first pebbles. The rockfall is natural, not a creature.
events.description: Small stones skittering down the slope. The big rocks follow the small ones.

onTriumph.narrative: Heard the first pebbles and moved camp to a sheltered overhang before anything fell.

onSuccess.narrative: Spotted the danger in time. Everyone clear, gear hauled to safety.

onMixed.narrative: Most of the camp is clear, but the scramble to move cost everyone sleep.

onFailure.narrative: A rock clips through the edge of camp. Catches someone a glancing blow. Gear scattered across the slope.

events.name: Mountain Dawn
events.gmPrompt: The sun breaks over the ridge and the whole valley floods with gold. For a few minutes, the world below is a sea of cloud and the camp sits above all of it.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. A reward for camping at altitude. The view is genuinely stunning. If a character paints, sketches, or journals, this is a natural prompt. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: The sun breaks over the ridge. The valley floods with gold.

events.name: Brazier Sparks
events.gmPrompt: The iron brazier on the corner pops and spits. A gust catches the embers and scatters them across the awning above the camp. Small. Manageable. If someone is watching.
events.checkContext: Can the watch spot and stamp out the sparks before they catch?
events.gmGuidance: A brazier or street lamp, not a building fire. No heroic call to action. The party does not need to save anyone -- they need to protect their own gear. Small scale, local consequence.
events.description: The iron brazier pops and spits. Embers scatter across the awning above camp.

onTriumph.narrative: Every spark stamped out before it caught. The watch adjusted the awning. Routine.

onSuccess.narrative: Close eye on the sparks. Caught them all. No damage.

onMixed.narrative: One ember caught on a pack strap. Stamped out quickly, but the smell of singed leather lingered.

effects.description: Anxiety from nearby fire kept sleep light.

onFailure.narrative: An ember smouldered unnoticed on a pack. The smoke woke the camp.

events.name: Street Musician
events.gmPrompt: A lone fiddler playing in the alley below. Not good, not bad. Just someone passing the time, same as everyone else tonight. The melody drifts and fades between the buildings.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. Urban equivalent of crickets. The fiddler is not a plot hook unless the GM wants them to be. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: A lone fiddler in the alley below. The melody drifts and fades between the buildings.

events.name: Night Rain
events.gmPrompt: Rain on the cobblestones outside. Steady, unhurried, washing the day off the city. The gutters sing. The area feels smaller. Safer.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: The rain is outside; the party is under cover. If camping in the open, this is pleasant background, not a hazard. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Rain on the cobblestones. Steady, unhurried. The gutters sing.

events.name: Stray Dog
events.gmPrompt: A thin dog has followed the party to their rest spot. It whines at the door. Scratches. Whines again. It is not going away on its own.
events.checkContext: Can the watch settle the dog before it disrupts the camp?
events.gmGuidance: Not a threat, not a plot hook. Just a hungry stray testing the party's patience. If a player wants to adopt it, that is entirely player-driven. The DC is low because this is a simple problem, not a hard one.
events.description: A thin dog whines at the door. It is not going away on its own.

onTriumph.narrative: A scrap of food and a firm hand. The dog settles at the threshold, quiet and grateful. Might even bark if something approaches.

onSuccess.narrative: Fed and calmed. It curls up outside and sleeps. No further noise.

onMixed.narrative: It took a while to settle. The whining woke half the camp before it gave up.

onFailure.narrative: Nothing works. It howls intermittently until dawn. The neighbours are not happy either.

effects.description: No rest with a howling dog outside.

events.name: Scavenger Pack
events.gmPrompt: Sharp yips from the dunes. Desert jackals circling, low and fast. They are testing the perimeter.
events.checkContext: Can the watch drive off the scavengers before they reach the food?
events.gmGuidance: Jackals are scavengers, not predators. The default check is Intimidation (noise and posturing). However, if players want to use Animal Handling (calm/redirect), a spell (Minor Illusion, Prestidigitation, Speak with Animals), or just weapons -- let them. Any reasonable approach substitutes for the check. The jackals flee from real resistance. This is about resourcefulness, not a specific skill.
events.description: Desert jackals circling the camp. They are testing the perimeter.

onTriumph.narrative: Saw their eyes in the firelight first. A thrown rock and a sharp whistle. They never gathered courage. Food stores untouched.

onSuccess.narrative: Noise and thrown objects. They scatter into the dark, yelping. Food stores untouched.

onMixed.narrative: Most scattered, but one bold one snatched from the edge of the food pile before fleeing.

onFailure.narrative: Multiple darted in at once. A ration bag dragged out of reach before anyone could grab it.

events.name: False Dawn
events.gmPrompt: The horizon shimmer starts early tonight. A lake that is not there. Trees that do not exist. The desert lies with total conviction and without any shame at all.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. The desert is beautiful and dishonest. If a player tries to investigate the mirage, it dissolves as they approach. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: The horizon shimmers. A lake that is not there. The desert lies without shame.

events.name: Cold Snap
events.gmPrompt: The temperature plummets after sundown. Breath frosts. Fingers stiffen. The sand gives up its heat without a fight.
events.checkContext: Can the party prepare for the cold before it settles in?
events.gmGuidance: Desert cold is real and catches the unprepared. Endure Elements or similar magic trivialises this entirely. No item loss, just recovery impact.
events.description: The temperature plummets. Breath frosts. The sand gives up its heat without a fight.

onTriumph.narrative: Layered clothing, heated stones in the bedrolls, fire banked right. Warm all night.

onSuccess.narrative: Cold but manageable. Adequate preparation.

onMixed.narrative: Shivering through the small hours. Stiff and tired by dawn.

onFailure.narrative: Bitterly cold. Muscles cramped, joints aching, rest ruined.

events.name: Dung Beetles
events.gmPrompt: Small armoured beetles rolling their prizes across the sand in the moonlight. Dozens of them, each one absolutely committed to its task. The desert runs a tight operation.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. Mundane, harmless, and oddly charming. A moment of quiet humour in a harsh environment. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Small armoured beetles rolling their prizes across the sand in the moonlight.

events.name: Shifting Ground
events.gmPrompt: The ground beneath camp is not as solid as it looked. A section drops six inches during the night, tilting toward the water. Gear slides toward the new low point.
events.checkContext: Can the watch notice the settling and move gear to higher ground?
events.gmGuidance: Survival to read the ground. escalate without a watch because nobody notices until morning. The sinkhole is slow, not sudden. Gear slides into standing water, gets waterlogged.
events.description: The ground is settling. Gear slides toward a new low point near the water.

onTriumph.narrative: You felt the subtle shift in the ground early. Everything moved to solid rock before the first puddle formed. Camp stayed dry all night.

onSuccess.narrative: You noticed the settling early and moved the packs to higher ground. Nothing lost.

onMixed.narrative: Caught the shift late. Some gear at the edge of camp is damp. An uncomfortable night.

effects.narrative: Gear at the camp edge dampened by shifting ground.

onFailure.narrative: Gear slid into standing water overnight. Boots, bedrolls, and a pack soaked through.

events.name: Frog Chorus
events.gmPrompt: The frogs start at sundown and they do not stop. Hundreds of them, each convinced it is the loudest. The swamp has its own orchestra and it plays all night whether you bought a ticket or not.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Not a sleep hazard -- just the ambient soundtrack of a swamp. Characters from rural backgrounds would find this normal. City characters might find it maddening, but mechanically it is harmless. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Hundreds of frogs, each convinced it is the loudest. The swamp plays all night.

events.name: Peat Bed Discovery
events.gmPrompt: While gathering firewood, someone stumbles upon a rare patch of relatively dry peat near the edge of camp. An incredibly valuable find in this sodden environment. Proper fuel that burns slow and hot.
events.checkContext: Can the watch harvest and ignite the peat effectively?
events.gmGuidance: Reward event. Peat is excellent fuel in a swamp where everything else is wet. The discovery itself is free -- the check is whether they can get it lit. Even failure is not punishing (just a wet disappointment). Only fires if watch is set.
events.description: A rare patch of dry peat near the edge of camp. Proper fuel in a sodden place.

onTriumph.narrative: You harvested the peat in clean blocks and built a slow-burn. It burned hot and steady all night. Gear dried, warmth soaked into cold bones.

onSuccess.narrative: The peat catches and burns slow and hot through the night. Everyone gets a chance to dry their gear.

onMixed.narrative: The peat burned, but unevenly. Some gear dried, some did not. A marginal improvement over the usual damp misery.

onFailure.narrative: Despite your best efforts, the peat is just too waterlogged to catch properly. It smoulders and smokes but never truly ignites. Worth the try.

events.name: Patient Hunter
events.gmPrompt: A grey heron stands motionless in the shallows at the edge of camp. It has been there since before first watch. It will be there after last. Waiting with a patience that borders on judgement.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: The heron catches a fish at some point during the night and swallows it whole. If a druid or ranger acknowledges it, that is a nice character moment. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: A grey heron stands motionless in the shallows. Waiting with a patience that borders on judgement.

events.name: Warm Hearth
events.gmPrompt: The fire crackles in the grate. The area smells of woodsmoke and old oak. The blankets are thick. Nobody is trying to kill you. That alone is worth the coin.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Pure comfort. The simplest possible tavern event. A warm room, a locked door, a quiet night. If the party has been in the wilds for multiple sessions, this hits harder than any mechanical bonus. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: The fire crackles. The blankets are thick. Nobody is trying to kill you.

events.name: The Snorer
events.gmPrompt: Someone in the next room snores like a wounded bear being dragged over gravel. The walls are thin. The snoring is not.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Comedy. Not mechanically punishing, just annoying. The snorer is a stranger and the party will never see them again. If a player character snores canonically, this is a natural callback. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Someone in the next room snores like a wounded bear. The walls are thin.

events.name: Early Kitchen
events.gmPrompt: The smell of fresh bread and frying bacon drifts up from the kitchen below. The innkeeper is already at work. A door creaks. Pans clatter. The day starts whether you are ready or not.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Warm atmosphere and a reminder that the world moves around the party. If the party goes downstairs, hot food is available (player-driven, outside this event). Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Fresh bread and frying bacon from the kitchen below. The innkeeper is already at work.

events.name: Roof Cats
events.gmPrompt: Scrabbling on the roof tiles above. A yowl. A thud. Silence. Then more scrabbling. The local cats are settling their own disputes up there, loudly and with conviction.
events.checkContext: No check. Ambient event.
events.gmGuidance: Comedy atmosphere. Territorial cats, not supernatural. The noise is intermittent and distracting but not sleep-ruining. If a player character has a cat familiar, the familiar's opinion on the roof drama is excellent RP material. Rest undisturbed.
events.description: Cats on the roof tiles above, settling disputes loudly and with conviction.

events.name: Oppressive Heat Wave
events.description: The air is thick and hot. Even at night, the temperature refuses to drop. The camp swelters.

mechanical.prompt: The heat is unbearable. Nobody can sleep. What does the party do?

onSuccess.narrative: You find a stream and set up near the water. The air is slightly cooler and everyone manages some rest.

onFailure.narrative: The march in darkness exhausts you further. You find water but arrive spent.

options.label: Stay Put and Endure the Heat

onSuccess.narrative: A miserable night, but you tough it out. The dawn brings cooler air.

onFailure.narrative: The heat takes its toll. Dehydration and exhaustion set in.

events.name: Biting Plague
events.description: A biblical cloud of biting insects descends on the camp. This is not a swarm. This is a plague.

mechanical.prompt: The insect cloud is so thick it blocks starlight. Bites cover exposed skin within seconds.

options.label: Build Smoky Fires to Drive Them Off

onSuccess.narrative: Green wood produces thick smoke that pushes the swarm back. You choke but the biting stops.

onFailure.narrative: The smoke helps but not enough. The biting continues through the night.

onSuccess.narrative: The mud dries to a protective crust. Uncomfortable but effective. The insects lose interest.

onFailure.narrative: Not enough mud to go around. Those without protection suffer badly.

events.name: Torrential Downpour
events.description: Rain hammers down so hard it stings exposed skin. Within minutes the ground is a river of mud.

mechanical.prompt: The rain is deafening. Tents are collapsing. The campfire is out. Visibility is zero.

onSuccess.narrative: Lashed tarps and braced frames hold against the storm. The camp survives battered but functional.

onFailure.narrative: The wind tears through your repairs. By dawn, everything is soaked through.

onSuccess.narrative: A rocky overhang provides dry shelter. The party waits out the storm in relative comfort.

onFailure.narrative: You wander in the dark rain for an hour before giving up and huddling under trees.

events.name: Spreading Fire
events.description: Smoke billows from the next street over. The wind shifts and a wall of flame rounds the corner, licking at the building you are sheltering in.

mechanical.prompt: The fire is spreading fast. Embers land on the roof above you. The street is filling with panicked crowds.

onSuccess.narrative: You push through the crowd and reach an open square well clear of the blaze. Your gear is intact.

onFailure.narrative: The crush of people separates the party. You stumble through smoke-choked alleys.
onFailure.prompt: Separated in the chaos, each of you must find your own way out.

onSuccess.narrative: Leaping between buildings, you spot the rest of the party in the square below. Singed but whole.

onFailure.narrative: A roof beam gives way. You tumble through and barely escape the collapsing building.

onSuccess.narrative: The sewers are foul but fireproof. You emerge blocks away, stinking but alive.

onFailure.narrative: You get turned around in the dark. The smoke follows you down. Eventually you find an exit, gasping.

onSuccess.narrative: Shoulder to shoulder with locals, you beat the fire back before it reaches your building. The neighbourhood owes you a debt.

onFailure.narrative: The fire is too large. Your efforts buy time but the building is lost.

events.name: Ceiling Collapse
events.description: A deep rumble shakes the walls. Dust pours from the ceiling as cracks spider across the stone above. The supports are failing.

mechanical.prompt: Chunks of rock are falling. The corridor behind you is already blocked. The ceiling is coming down.

onSuccess.narrative: You dive through a shower of stone and slide into a stable chamber beyond. Covered in dust but alive.

onFailure.narrative: Heavy stone pins one of you. The corridor is sealed.
onFailure.prompt: A party member is trapped under rubble. The ceiling is still groaning.

onSuccess.narrative: Straining together, you lift the slab and pull them free. Battered but breathing.

onFailure.narrative: Another section gives way during the rescue attempt. Everyone takes a beating.

onSuccess.narrative: A narrow side passage leads around the blockage. You reach your companion from the other side and pry them loose.

onFailure.narrative: The detour leads deeper into unstable tunnels. More cave-ins slow you and the trapped party member suffers.

options.label: Brace the Ceiling with Camp Equipment

onSuccess.narrative: Tent poles, shields, and a door frame hold just long enough. The collapse passes and you are still standing.

onFailure.narrative: Your makeshift supports buckle. Rocks rain down on everyone.

effects.description: Equipment crushed or used as bracing.

events.name: Sinking Camp
events.description: The ground beneath the camp is giving way. The peat was too soft. Tents are sinking and everything is sliding into the mire.

mechanical.prompt: The earth is swallowing the camp. Water and mud are bubbling up. You have minutes before everything sinks.

onSuccess.narrative: You drag what you can to a rocky outcrop. Half the camp is gone but the party is safe.

onFailure.narrative: The saturated ground pulls at your boots. You lose your footing and slide into the murk.

onSuccess.narrative: Working methodically, you lift each pack from the sucking mud. Slow work, but little is lost.

onFailure.narrative: The sinking accelerates. You lose half your supplies trying to save all of them.

effects.description: No rest, spent the night relocating.

events.name: Resonant Chamber
events.description: The room hums. Deep enough to feel in the chest. The stone breathes in its own way.
